banner.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1200 360" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><defs><linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ff9f1c"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ff7b00"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="1200" height="360" fill="url(#g)"/><text x="60" y="150" font-size="56" font-weight="900" fill="white">Saturday Questions ❓</text><text x="60" y="210" font-size="32" font-weight="700" fill="white">Sorting Trace & Algorithm Detection</text><text x="60" y="260" font-size="20" fill="white">Understand how arrays change step by step</text></svg>

questions.title: Bubble Sort Trace
questions.prompt:
Perform Bubble Sort and show the array after every pass.

In Bubble Sort, adjacent elements are swapped and the largest element moves to the end in each pass.
hints: Largest element settles at the end

questions.title: Selection Sort Trace
questions.prompt:
Perform Selection Sort and show the array after each pass.

Each pass selects the minimum element and places it at the correct position.
hints: Find minimum element in unsorted part

questions.title: Insertion Sort Trace
questions.prompt:
Perform Insertion Sort and show how the sorted portion grows.

At each step, insert the current element into its correct position in the sorted left part.
hints: Take one element at a time
Shift larger elements to the right

questions.title: Identify Sorting Algorithm
questions.prompt:
Given the following transformations, identify the sorting algorithm used.

Start: [5,1,4,2,8]
Pass1: [1,4,2,5,8]
Pass2: [1,2,4,5,8]
Pass3: [1,2,4,5,8]
hints: Largest moving to end indicates Bubble Sort

questions.title: Compare Sorting Algorithms
questions.prompt:
Given an array, compare Bubble, Selection, and Insertion Sort.

Answer:
1. Which performs the most swaps?
2. Which works best for nearly sorted arrays?
3. Which always performs the same number of comparisons?

examples.output:
Bubble: many swaps
Insertion: best for nearly sorted
Selection: fixed comparisons
